title: Radioactive Waste Disposal
desc: We evaluate the feasibility of nuclear waste disposal via solar impact versus asteroid belt storage. The required $\Delta v$ to reach the asteroid belt $(6.274 	ext{ km/s})$ is lower than the requirements for direct solar impact $(29.8 	ext{ km/s})$ or a Jupiter swing-by $(7.174 	ext{ km/s})$. Furthermore, the calculated collision frequency in the asteroid belt is negligible $(3.49 	imes 10^{17} 	ext{ years} / 	ext{km}^2)$. Due to these lower orbital energy requirements and minimal collision risk, we conclude that asteroid belt storage presents a safer and more viable disposal method.

title: Relativistic Dynamics of Electromagnetic Two Body Problem
desc: The electromagnetic interaction of two classical point charges is investigated with special relativistic rigor. Numerical integration of their equations of motion yields anomalous, unphysical solutions. We discuss the mathematical origins of these discrepancies and outline the analytical considerations necessary to recover physically allowable trajectories.

title: Neutrino Oscillation
desc: Wigner Monte Carlo simulation of neutrino flavor state superpositions propagating through space, tracking the phase shift governed by the PMNS matrix.

title: Ising Model Simulation
desc: Simulation of spontaneous symmetry breaking in a 2D simple square grid, with periodic boundary conditions, using the Metropolis-Hastings algorithm with the functionality to calculate different observables like magnetization, specific heat and susceptibility at different temperatures. \ \ Currently planning to extend the simulation to different lattice structures with advanced algorithms (e.g., Wolf-Cluster algorithm) having the objective to understand criticality with conformal map.

title: Inverse Ising Problem (Gradient Descent)
desc: Simulation of spontaneous symmetry breaking and the divergence of correlation length near a critical temperature in a 3D lattice array.
